conditions: Closest 4 ft piling on right is sharp with clear texture. The 11 ft and 14 ft pilings show good structure. This matches conditions where 30 ft pilings are faint silhouettes.

Bottom is clearly visible. The nearer pilings (4 ft and 11 ft) have strong texture; the 14 ft piling on the left is present but somewhat hazy. The far 30 ft pilings are not distinctly visible as silhouettes. This matches conditions where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded, indicating about 15 ft visibility.

Closest and mid pilings are sharp with texture. The back-left/center pilings around 30 ft are visible but only as soft silhouettes, not sharp. Sandy bottom is visible but hazy. This matches the ~25 ft reference more than the 30–35 ft cases.

Closest 4ft piling on right is sharp with clear texture. The 11ft piling on right and 14ft piling on left are visible but very hazy silhouettes with little detail. Farthest 30ft pilings are not discernible. This matches the guideline where the 14ft piling is hazy/faded silhouette.

The closest 4 ft piling (right edge) is very sharp, and the 11 ft piling on the mid-right is clearly visible with texture. The 14 ft piling on the left is present but quite hazy with limited detail, and there is no clear view of the 30 ft pilings. Sandy bottom is visible but water column is notably murky. Based on the guideline that a hazy/faded 14 ft piling corresponds to ~15 ft visibility, this frame matches that condition.

Closest 4ft piling at right is clear with texture. The 11ft/14ft pilings appear only as very hazy silhouettes with minimal detail, and farther 30ft pilings are not discernible. This corresponds to conditions where the 14ft piling is a faded silhouette and farther structures are effectively lost in the haze.

Closest 4ft and 11ft pilings are very clear with good texture. The 14ft/back-left piling is visible but somewhat hazy; farther 30ft pilings are not distinctly detectable. This corresponds best to the 14ft piling being hazy/faded, matching ~15 ft guideline.
